skill.name: comparison-table-gen
skill.description: Auto-generates comparison tables for concepts, drugs, or study results.
skill.summary: Generates markdown comparison tables from comma-separated inputs for medical research and evidence synthesis.
skill.author: AIPOCH

security_audit.summary: All 14 high-severity static findings are false positives: 'Weak cryptographic algorithm' detections are noise from non-crypto content in documentation and audit metadata; 'Path traversal sequence' findings reference a security checklist discussing what to prevent, not actual path traversal; 'System reconnaissance' findings match evaluation metadata and feature descriptions only. The 32 'Ruby/shell backtick execution' findings are markdown code block formatting, not executable shell commands. One true positive file write at scripts/main.py:99 writes comparison output to a user-specified file path, which is the intended behavior of this CLI tool. The hardcoded URL in SKILL.md is a source attribution to the skill's own GitHub repository. No malicious intent, no data exfiltration, and no prompt injection attempts detected.

medium_findings.title: Python File Write Operation
medium_findings.description: Script writes comparison table output to a user-specified file path using open() with write mode. This is the intended and documented behavior of the CLI tool. The output path is controlled by the user via command-line argument. No path traversal or unauthorized file access is performed.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URL in Documentation
low_findings.description: SKILL.md contains a markdown link to the skill's source repository (github.com/aipoch/medical-research-skills). This is a source attribution and not a suspicious network call. The skill makes no actual HTTP requests.

low_findings.title: Path Traversal References in Security Checklist
low_findings.description: SKILL.md line 140 and 143 reference '../' and 'path traversal' within a security checklist section that describes patterns to validate against. These are documentation-only references to security best practices, not actual path traversal code.

content.user_title: Generate Medical Comparison Tables in Seconds
content.value_statement: Researchers spend hours manually creating comparison tables for drugs, treatments, and study results. This skill auto-generates formatted comparison tables from simple comma-separated inputs, saving time and ensuring consistent output.
actual_capabilities: Generate side-by-side comparison tables from comma-separated items and attributes
Output comparison data as clean markdown tables for documentation and reports
Support drug comparison workflows with customizable comparison fields
Save comparison results to JSON files for further processing
Process multiple comparison attributes simultaneously for comprehensive analysis
Handle command-line arguments for automated and scripted workflows
limitations: Cannot research or populate table cell content; only generates table structure
Does not rank or score compared items; provides neutral structure only
Requires comma-separated inputs without support for CSV or spreadsheet imports
Output is limited to markdown and JSON formats only

use_cases.title: Drug Mechanism Comparison
use_cases.description: Compare multiple drugs across mechanisms of action, dosage, and side effects for research documentation.

use_cases.title: Treatment Option Analysis
use_cases.description: Generate comparison tables for treatment options across cost, efficacy, and recovery time.

use_cases.title: Study Result Comparison
use_cases.description: Create structured comparisons of study methodologies and outcomes for systematic reviews.

prompt_templates.title: Basic Drug Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a comparison table for Drug A, Drug B, and Drug C across Mechanism, Dosage, and Side Effects.

prompt_templates.title: Treatment Comparison with File Output
prompt_templates.prompt: Generate a comparison table for Surgery, Chemotherapy, and Radiation. Compare across Cost, Efficacy, Recovery Time, and Risk Level. Save the result to treatment-comparison.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: User comparing treatment options for patient education

prompt_templates.title: Study Methodology Comparison
prompt_templates.prompt: Create a comparison table for Study A, Study B, and Study C. Compare across Sample Size, Methodology, Outcome Measures, Follow-up Period, and Statistical Significance. Save to study-comparison.json.
prompt_templates.scenario: Researcher comparing multiple clinical studies for a systematic review

prompt_templates.title: Comprehensive Research Synthesis Table
prompt_templates.prompt: I need to compare three intervention approaches across 8 attributes including patient population, intervention protocol, primary endpoint, secondary endpoints, adverse events, follow-up duration, funding source, and publication year. Generate the comparison table structure and save to intervention-comparison.json. Then help me populate each cell with available data from my notes.
prompt_templates.scenario: Senior researcher conducting a comprehensive evidence synthesis

output_examples.input: Compare Drug A and Drug B across Mechanism, Dose, and Side Effects
output_examples.output: A markdown comparison table with Drug A and Drug B as column headers and Mechanism, Dose, and Side Effects as row labels. The table uses standard markdown formatting with pipe separators and dashed header dividers, ready for insertion into documentation or research notes.

output_examples.input: Compare Surgery, Chemotherapy, and Radiation across Cost, Efficacy, and Recovery Time. Save the output to treatment-comparison.json.
output_examples.output: A markdown table with three treatment columns and three attribute rows, plus a JSON file saved to disk containing the structured comparison with items array, attributes array, and markdown table string for downstream processing.

best_practices: Provide specific and distinct item names to avoid confusion in table headers
Use consistent attribute formatting across comparisons for clean and readable output
Save output to files when working with large comparison sets to avoid terminal truncation
anti_patterns: Using the skill to research or fill in comparison data it cannot access from external sources
Providing more than 10 comparison items which makes the table unreadable and difficult to scan
Assuming the skill can validate, rank, or score the accuracy of compared items

faq.question: What types of items can I compare?
faq.answer: You can compare any concepts, drugs, treatments, or study results. The skill creates the table structure from your comma-separated inputs.

faq.question: Does the skill research comparison data for me?
faq.answer: No. The skill generates the table structure only. You must provide the content for each cell.

faq.question: Can I save the output to a file?
faq.answer: Yes. Use the --output or -o flag with a file path to save the comparison table as a JSON file.

faq.question: What output formats are supported?
faq.answer: The skill outputs markdown tables displayed in the terminal and JSON files when an output path is specified.

faq.question: How many items can I compare at once?
faq.answer: There is no hard limit, but tables with more than 10 items become difficult to read. We recommend comparing 2 to 6 items.

faq.question: Is this skill suitable for clinical decision-making?
faq.answer: No. This skill provides a table structure for organizing information. It should not be used as a substitute for clinical judgment or evidence-based practice guidelines.
